samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, the motor vehicle lane light should be green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. Only the lane light is considered in this case.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1_red">State 1 - Red.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3_green">State 3 - Green.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>
                The state machine transitions between states based on the internal counter and the pedestrian button. 
                In the idle state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state. 
                In the s1_red state, it transitions to the s3_green state after the counter reaches 60. 
                In the s3_green state, it transitions to the s2_yellow state after the counter reaches 10. 
                In the s2_yellow state, it transitions back to the s1_red state after the counter reaches 5. 
                The state machine can also transition to the s3_green state when the pedestrian button is pressed, 
                if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks. 
            </description>
            <state id="idle">
                <description>Transition to s1_red state.</description>
                <transition id="s1_red"/>
            </state>
            <state id="s1_red">
                <description>Transition to s3_green state after counter reaches 60.</description>
                <transition id="s3_green" condition="cnt == 60"/>
                <transition id="s1_red"/>
            </state>
            <state id="s2_yellow">
                <description>Transition to s1_red state after counter reaches 5.</description>
                <transition id="s1_red" condition="cnt == 5"/>
                <transition id="s2_yellow"/>
            </state>
            <state id="s3_green">
                <description>Transition to s2_yellow state after counter reaches 10.</description>
                <transition id="s2_yellow" condition="cnt == 10"/>
                <transition id="s3_green"/>
            </state>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The traffic light controller logic is implemented using three always blocks. 
            The first always block is for state transitions, the second for counter logic, 
            and the third for assigning output values to the red, yellow, and green signals. 
        </description>
        <always>
            <description>
                The state transition logic is implemented using a case statement. 
                The state machine transitions between states based on the internal counter and the pedestrian button. 
                In the idle state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state. 
                In the s1_red state, it transitions to the s3_green state after the counter reaches 60. 
                In the s3_green state, it transitions to the s2_yellow state after the counter reaches 10. 
                In the s2_yellow state, it transitions back to the s1_red state after the counter reaches 5. 
                The state machine can also transition to the s3_green state when the pedestrian button is pressed, 
                if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks. 
            </description>
            <case subject="state">
                <case_item id="idle">
                    <description>Transition to s1_red state.</description>
                    <transition id="s1_red"/>
                </case_item>
                <case_item id="s1_red">
                    <description>Transition to s3_green state after counter reaches 60.</description>
                    <transition id="s3_green" condition="cnt == 60"/>
                    <transition id="s1_red"/>
                </case_item>
                <case_item id="s2_yellow">
                    <description>Transition to s1_red state after counter reaches 5.</description>
                    <transition id="s1_red" condition="cnt == 5"/>
                    <transition id="s2_yellow"/>
                </case_item>
                <case_item id="s3_green">
                    <description>Transition to s2_yellow state after counter reaches 10.</description>
                    <transition id="s2_yellow" condition="cnt == 10"/>
                    <transition id="s3_green"/>
                </case_item>
            </case>
        </always>
        <always>
            <description>
                The counter logic is implemented using a simple counter. 
                The counter is reset to 10 on the positive edge of the reset signal. 
                It is set to 60 when the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active. 
                It is set to 10 when the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active. 
                It is set to 5 when the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active. 
                It is set to 10 when the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active. 
                Otherwise, it is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock. 
            </description>
            <if condition="rst_n">
                <description>Reset counter to 10 on positive edge of reset signal.</description>
                <assign id="cnt" value="10"/>
            </if>
            <else>
                <description>
                    Check conditions for setting counter to 60, 10, 5, or 10. 
                    Otherwise, decrement counter by 1 on positive edge of clock. 
                </description>
                <if condition="pass_request && green">
                    <description>Set counter to 60 if pass_request signal is active and green signal is active.</description>
                    <assign id="cnt" value="60"/>
                </if>
                <else_if condition="!green && p_green">
                    <description>Set counter to 10 if green signal is inactive and previous green signal (p_green) was active.</description>
                    <assign id="cnt" value="10"/>
                </else_if>
                <else_if condition="!yellow && p_yellow">
                    <description>Set counter to 5 if yellow signal is inactive and previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active.</description>
                    <assign id="cnt" value="5"/>
                </else_if>
                <else_if condition="!red && p_red">
                    <description>Set counter to 10 if red signal is inactive and previous red signal (p_red) was active.</description>
                    <assign id="cnt" value="10"/>
                </else_if>
                <else>
                    <description>Decrement counter by 1 on positive edge of clock.</description>
                    <assign id="cnt" value="cnt - 1"/>
                </else>
            </else>
        </always>
        <always>
            <description>
                The output signals are assigned the values of the internal registers p_red, p_yellow, and p_green. 
                This assignment occurs on the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. 
                If the reset signal is active, the output signals are set to 0. 
                Otherwise, they are set to the values of the internal registers. 
            </description>
            <if condition="rst_n">
                <description>Set output signals to 0 if reset signal is active.</description>
                <assign id="red" value="0"/>
                <assign id="yellow" value="0"/>
                <assign id="green" value="0"/>
            </if>
            <else>
                <description>Assign internal registers to output signals.</description>
                <assign id="red" value="p_red"/>
                <assign id="yellow" value="p_yellow"/>
                <assign id="green" value="p_green"/>
            </else>
        </always>
    </implementation>
</module>
